AssignmentLease.description: Exclusive write lease for one delegate session.
AssignmentLease.title: AssignmentLease

Checkpoint.description: Durable delegate recovery evidence captured during execution.
Checkpoint.title: Checkpoint

ConnectionProfile.description:
Machine-readable statement of how to connect to this daemon (#996).

Published at ``GET /api/version`` adjacent metadata and importable by
consumers so the default port / health probe / version-negotiation
endpoint are a single source of truth instead of a hard-coded guess.
ConnectionProfile.title: ConnectionProfile

ControlPlaneActionView.description: Action available in the control plane.
ControlPlaneActionView.title: ControlPlaneActionView

ControlPlaneWorkItemView.description: View model for control plane work items.
ControlPlaneWorkItemView.title: ControlPlaneWorkItemView

CriticFindingView.description: Finding from the critic review process.
CriticFindingView.title: CriticFindingView

DelegateSession.description: Concrete execution session for a logical delegate.
DelegateSession.title: DelegateSession

DelegateSessionSnapshot.description: Durable readback for one delegate session and its evidence.
DelegateSessionSnapshot.title: DelegateSessionSnapshot

DelegateSessionStatus.description: Lifecycle status for one concrete delegate execution session.
DelegateSessionStatus.title: DelegateSessionStatus

GateCancelRequest.description: Request to cancel a queued gate.
GateCancelRequest.title: GateCancelRequest

GateRetryRequest.description: Request to retry a failed gate.
GateRetryRequest.title: GateRetryRequest

GateTimelineEntry.description: Timeline entry for a gate in the control plane.
GateTimelineEntry.title: GateTimelineEntry

GateWaiverRequest.description: Request to waive a failed gate.
GateWaiverRequest.title: GateWaiverRequest

GraphNode.description: One role invocation in a delivery graph.
GraphNode.title: GraphNode

HandoffArtifact.description: Typed evidence or work product handed from one delegate to another.
HandoffArtifact.title: HandoffArtifact

LeaseRecoveryPolicy.description: Recovery behavior once a session lease expires.
LeaseRecoveryPolicy.title: LeaseRecoveryPolicy

TaskGraph.description: Validated DAG of named sub-agent roles for one work item.
TaskGraph.title: TaskGraph

TaskSubmit.description:
Request model for task submission.

``extra="forbid"`` so unknown fields (e.g. a consumer forwarding
``confirmation_token`` / ``idempotency_key`` to this non-gated endpoint)
fail loudly with a 422 naming the key instead of being silently dropped.
The confirmation-gated, idempotent dispatch path is ``POST /api/dispatch``
(#994).

depends_on.description: Task IDs that must reach COMPLETED before this task starts.
depends_on.title: Depends On

prompt.description: Prompt text must be between 1 and 500,000 characters
prompt.title: Prompt

TaskSubmit.title: TaskSubmit

info.description:
Maxwell-Daemon exposes a stable HTTP + WebSocket API for orchestrating agent tasks, observing pipeline state, and controlling the daemon lifecycle.

**Contract version:** `2.0.0` (advertised at `GET /api/version`).  The contract is **append-only**: new fields and endpoints may appear, but existing request and response shapes are stable within a major version.

## Authentication

Most endpoints require a bearer token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your_token>
```

When JWT auth is configured, role-based access control (viewer / operator / admin) is enforced per endpoint.

## Interactive docs

* Swagger UI: [`/docs`](/docs)
* ReDoc: [`/redoc`](/redoc)
* OpenAPI JSON: [`/openapi.json`](/openapi.json)
info.summary: Autonomous AI control plane orchestrating agent tasks.
info.title: Maxwell-Daemon API

get.description:
Canonical, machine-readable connection profile (#996).

Consumers fetch this (or vendor ``contract.connection_profile()``) to
learn the default port / health + version endpoints / systemd unit
rather than guessing.

Receive GitHub webhook events.

Authenticated with HMAC-SHA256 via X-Hub-Signature-256. No bearer-token
dependency is applied so GitHub's retry delivery system isn't double-gated.

post.description: Trigger a task from any external source via a generic HTTP webhook.

get.description: Legacy liveness probe used by unit tests and some health checks.

Kubernetes-style liveness probe.

Returns ``{"status": "ready"}`` when the daemon is healthy and
backends are available, or ``{"status": "ok"}`` when alive but not
yet ready.  HTTP 200 in both cases.
Legacy readiness probe.
